channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Programming Competition on April 4th
item.description: Date: Saturday, April 4, 2026 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM - OPT-RPI, RPI’s yearly programming competition, is this weekend on April 4th. The competition will be in Lally 102 at 12:00 PM. Join for food, fun, and $2000 in prizes!
item.author: Students

item.title: Rensselaer Newman Theology Roundtable with Guest Speaker Fr. Mulcahy, OP
item.description: Join us Wednesday, April 9, 2026 at 7:30 p.m. to examine the development of 20th and 21st century Catholic teaching about migration, and to identify questions about the mass movements of peoples that often go unasked. How, if at all, does the large scale of contemporary migration change and challenge our hearing of the Gospel? All are welcome.
item.author: Community

item.title: The O.T. Sawson Multidisciplinary Design Lab announces New Director!
item.description: We are thrilled to announce the appointment of Professor Sandipan Mishra as the new Director of the O.T. Swanson Multidisciplinary Design Laboratory (The Design Lab), effective March 1, 2026. A distinguished faculty member in the Department of Mechanical, Aerospace, and Nuclear Engineering, Professor Mishra steps into this leadership role at a pivotal moment for engineering design at RPI.
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - Focus on Phishing Prevention Course
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 2nd, 2026 2:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Location: LOW Building 3116 - Please join us for our upcoming 30-minute course for Cybersecurity Awareness - Focus on Phishing Prevention. There will be snacks and beverages provided during the course.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty/Staff Memorial Service to be Held April 14
item.description: In remembrance of the individuals we lost this past year, the Pillars of Rensselaer invite all members of the Rensselaer community, including alumni and friends, to attend this year’s Faculty and Staff Memorial Service Faculty/Staff Memorial Service.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cognitive Science Colloquium with Dr. Ben Weissman scheduled for April 1st
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science Department on Wednesday, April 1st, at 12:00 pm in Carnegie 113 for a presentation titled “How we build linguistic representations in social and digital contexts” by Dr. Ben Weissman, Visiting Assistant Professor in the Psychology Department at College of the Holy Cross.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Spring Seminar Series: Edson Severnini, Boston College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Edson Severnini, who will present: “The Social Lifecycle Impacts of Power Plant Siting in the Historical United States” next Wednesday, April 1st at 3:30 PM in SAGE 5510.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Getting More Done Without Burning Out”
item.description: Imagine sprinting through a marathon of endless demands—where every step forward risks burnout unless you rethink your approach. Traditional productivity strategies can backfire, eroding focus, energy, and decision-making. Unlock sustainable productivity in Nabeela Elsayed’s Getting More Done Without Burning Out Leadercamp, and discover strategies to stay composed, focus on what matters, and inspire your team with clarity and confidence. Two times: 9:00A-10:00A & 12:00PM-1:00PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Fidelity Confidential Consultation (By Phone with Zoom Video Option)
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 2, 2026 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM - Schedule a confidential consultation to help you become better informed and more confident about your workplace retirement savings plan benefits. To schedule an appointment, call 1-800-642-7131, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to midnight, or online at https://nb.fidelity.com/public/nbpreloginnav/rpi/microsite/#/tem/meet-with-us
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Commencement Volunteer Opportunities
item.description:
Commencement is one of the best opportunities at RPI to connect with fellow faculty, staff, and our graduating students! To ensure a memorable experience for our graduating class, we need assistance from everyone. Please consider volunteering at commencement or finding other ways to support the event leading up to the big day. Please click the title to access the online form and confirm your participation with a manager beforehand.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
